source: World Bank Environmental and Social Framework, ESF Guidance Note on Classification, 2018. Risk classification is project-specific and determined by the World Bank. Criteria shown are illustrative for road projects.
framework: World Bank Environmental and Social Framework (ESF), 2018

high.description: Projects with potential for significant adverse environmental and social risks and impacts that are diverse, irreversible, or unprecedented.

criteria_for_road_projects.criterion: New road through protected area, national park, or critical natural habitat
Significant involuntary resettlement (>200 persons physically displaced)
Impacts on Indigenous Peoples / historically underserved traditional local communities
Major infrastructure in conflict-affected or fragile areas
Project requiring significant cultural heritage site relocation or destruction

high.world_bank_review: Prior review at all stages. No-objection required for TOR, draft ESIA, final ESIA, ESMP, RAP.

substantial.description: Projects with potential for significant adverse environmental and social risks and impacts that are fewer in number, generally site-specific, largely reversible, and can be mitigated.

criteria_for_road_projects.criterion: Road upgrade with limited land acquisition (<200 persons, no physical relocation)
Road near but not through sensitive ecosystem (within 1 km of wetland, forest reserve)
Road through areas with significant community safety concerns (dense pedestrian activity)
Large-scale quarrying or borrow pit operations required

substantial.world_bank_review: Prior review for key instruments. Selective no-objection.

moderate.description: Projects with limited adverse environmental and social risks and impacts that are few in number, generally site-specific, largely reversible, and readily mitigated.

criteria_for_road_projects.criterion: Road rehabilitation within existing corridor with no land acquisition
Minor road widening within existing road reserve (no resettlement)
Bridge rehabilitation at existing location with temporary construction impacts

low.description: Projects with minimal or negligible adverse environmental and social risks and impacts.

criteria_for_road_projects.criterion: Routine road maintenance with no physical works outside existing footprint
Road safety improvements (signs, markings, speed humps) with no land acquisition
Technical assistance, studies, or institutional strengthening with no physical works

required_instruments: Standard contractual environmental and social provisions

notes: Risk classification is determined by the World Bank, not the borrower.
Classification can be revised upward or downward during project preparation if scope changes.
ESS 2 (Labor) and ESS 10 (Stakeholder Engagement) apply to virtually all projects with physical works.
These criteria are illustrative. Each project is classified individually based on its specific circumstances.
For Uganda specifically: ESS 7 applies to projects affecting Batwa (southwest), Ik (northeast), and potentially Karamojong pastoralists.
